headline: JP 30Y breaches 4 — first since 1999
deck: Auction calendar is the binding constraint, not the photo-op. Three of five forecasting agents flagged intervention risk above 158.
body: Japan's 30Y yield crossed 4.01% at the Friday Tokyo close — the first crossing since 1999. The weekly delta is +12bp; the path is the third week in a row that the long-end has led intermediate tenors.

Foreman flagged intervention risk above the 158 USD/JPY threshold; quorum agrees but with two dissents who cite prior MoF inaction at 161. The desk reads the auction calendar as the binding constraint, not the BoJ press conference.

Position book: Foreman entered short JGB futures via 5-7y the morning before the print and has carried through the weekend. The desk's modal scenario for Monday Asian open: MoF verbal escalation but no operational action; risk-off bleeds into equity beta.

evidence_box.fragment: 4.01% at the Tokyo close — first print above 4 since 1999.
December hike pricing at 0.67 — was 0.12 three months ago.
Foreman's long-end call and stop levels, filed Friday.
